retailer_feedback: Customer wanted lime pickle flavor chips. Described exact taste they want in detail.
Customer said bigger pack wasteful for solo eating. Small pack is perfect portion for one.
Retailer said normal salt-and-pepper chips not moving. Fusion flavors going fast.
Customers asking for dipping sauces along with chips. Western eating style clear.
Customers asking for village-style snacks. Want rustic, handmade-looking packaging.
Owner told me yoga class nearby sending customers. All asking for light, natural snacks.
Retailer said if we don't launch extra hot soon, he'll give that shelf to competitor.
Customer asking if roasted peanuts qualify as protein snack. Wants healthy option.
Customer complained our gift packs look ordinary compared to competitor. Lost the sale.
Owner near tech park said project completion celebrations include snack gifting internally.
Customer asked for 10-pack multipack of single-serves. Wants weekly office snack supply.
Retailer told me small packs drive more brand trials than any sampling activity.
Kids and teenagers asking for extra sour options. Said regular flavors boring.
Customer asked if our product uses palm oil. Environmental concern driving plant-based choice.
Retailer said plant-based demand strongest among young educated women in this area.
Retailer near nature cure center said their visitors only buy plant-based without exception.
Owner said traditional mixture buyers are old generation. New customers want western.
Owner told me during salary delay week, customers only buy rupees 5 packs. Very visible shift.
Asked retailer what's trending this month - first thing he said was spicy, without thinking.
Customer asked for variety gift pack with 6 different flavors inside. Couldn't provide.
Viral reel showing snack hack - customers came same evening asking for specific product.
Owner just chatted casually, no specific consumer trend mentioned
Doctor clinic next door sending patients - all asking for low sugar snack options.
Retailer said yogurt and tamarind combo flavor is new experimental request from customers.
Customer comparing our product to an international brand. Said theirs tastes more western.
Fitness group in area active on WhatsApp - owner gets recommendations flow from there.
Owner near airport said travelers want small packs within carry-on limits.
Owner near coworking space said freelancers snacking all day. Convenience format perfect.
Festival order came for 150 pieces with branding on pack. Big opportunity missed.
Owner told me tangy snacks moving fast near movie theaters. People eating during interval.
Owner said government offices also started gifting snack hampers during national celebrations.
Health-conscious buyers checking expiry and ingredients both. Very careful now.
Owner switched prime shelf position to spicy variants. Said mild doesn't justify the space.
Owner told me green mango flavor appeals to all ages. Widest appeal in his store.
Retailer asked me if brand runs influencer campaigns. Said competitor does and it works.
Customers asking if we have portion-sized packs. Said big pack goes stale before finishing.
Corporate health drive - office employees checking sugar. Retailer seeing pattern.
Owner said regional snacks gifted during festival by many customers. Local pride visible.
Customers only buying small Rs.5 packs, big packs not selling at all.
Young office crowd asking for protein bars and high protein namkeen daily.
